title: clypeola aspera
intro: Clypeola aspera, also known as the Indian turnip or hairy cleome, is a plant species that belongs to the Cleomaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Clypeola aspera include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and treat digestive issues. Clypeola aspera can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
